name: shodan-skill

documentation.readme: An advanced, all-in-one Shodan wrapper skill for AI agents running on OpenClaw.
usage_sections: Features: - 🔎 Advanced Search: Full support for search filters (vuln:, port:, etc.) and facets (statistics). - 📊 Trends Analysis: Generate statistics on global asset distribution (e.g., top countries using Nginx). - 🔬 Host Intelligence: Deep inspection of IP addresses, including open ports

Installation: Prerequisites OpenClaw: Ensure you have OpenClaw installed and running. Python 3: The skill relies on Python 3 environment. Shodan Library: You need to install the official python library. 1. Install Skill Clone this repository into your OpenClaw skills directory (usually /.openc

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_reconnaissance_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Reconnaissance category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_reconnaissance_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Reconnaissance system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_reference_implementation_when_designing_a_new_v3_method_for_this_category
function_signatures.description: Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_teaching_example_in_the_prompt_stanza_so_the_llm_can_reason_about_the_right_run_order
function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for shodan-skill (liuweitao/shodan-skill). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for shodan-skill. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with xransum/phantex (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with smoorthi_uno/Shodan_Python_Project (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with sergioamsilva/wifi-security-toolkit (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web, cloud, iots] in the Reconnaissance category. Fits when the engagement needs: Shodan Skill for OpenClaw.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: An advanced, all-in-one Shodan wrapper skill for AI agents running on OpenClaw.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/liuweitao/shodan-skill.git ~/.openclaw/skills/shodan-skill; pip3 install shodan. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `mkdir -p ~/.openclaw/skills`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
